commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an Android Gateway Talk setup-readiness parser, exposes it through NodeRuntime/MainViewModel, uses it in Voice and Voice Setup, preserves realtime close failure status, and adds focused Android unit tests.

PR surface: Other +554. Total +554 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? No live emulator reproduction was performed in this read-only review, but current-main source inspection shows Voice and Voice Setup use separate static/status-derived readiness while Gateway exposes the mixed-readiness talk.catalog contract described by the linked issue.

target.title: fix: unify Android Voice setup readiness
target.body:
Closes #98268

AI-assisted: yes. Sanitized transcript logs were not included.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Android users opening Voice, Realtime Talk, Dictation, or Voice Setup could see inconsistent configured/unconfigured state when Gateway Talk catalog readiness differed between realtime voice and realtime transcription. It also resolves a problem where Talk/Dictation startup failures could leave only clipped status text instead of durable setup guidance or readable provider details.

## Why This Change Was Made

Android now derives Realtime Talk and Dictation readiness from the Gateway `talk.catalog` / `talk.config` contract through one shared readiness model used by both the Voice tab and Voice Setup. Startup/provider failures stay on Voice with a persistent issue panel, setup action, and sanitized expandable details, without changing Gateway/provider implementation or unrelated Android onboarding/settings flows.

## User Impact

Users now see the same nouns and configured truth on Voice and Voice Setup: Realtime Talk can need setup while Dictation is ready, and both surfaces say so consistently. When provider startup fails, users stay in the Voice flow and can open setup or expand the sanitized details instead of getting bounced back with a clipped one-line error.

## Evidence

- Focused Android unit tests passed:

```shell
JAVA_HOME='/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home' ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ANDROID_SDK_ROOT="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" ./gradlew --no-daemon :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.GatewayTalkSetupReadinessTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.ui.VoiceScreenLogicTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest
# BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 2m 26s
# 30 actionable tasks: 30 executed
```

- Whitespace check passed:

```shell
git diff --check
```

- Local emulator proof captured on `OpenClaw_FinalProof_API_36` with the Play debug app installed:
  - Voice tab showed `Realtime Talk needs setup · Dictation ready`, persistent setup panel, and `Open Voice Setup` / `Set Up Talk` actions.
  - Voice Setup showed the same Gateway catalog rows: `Realtime Talk` as `Needs setup`, `Dictation` as `Ready`.
  - A short screen recording was captured showing navigation between Voice and Voice Setup.

- Local artifact directory was scanned for obvious secret-shaped strings; no matches were found. Visual artifacts were kept outside the repository per PR artifact policy and were not committed.
